title: erodium triangulare subsp. bovei
intro: Erodium triangulare subsp. bovei, also known as the annual heron's bill, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the geranium family, known for its red or pink flowers and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Erodium triangulare subsp. bovei include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant's leaves have been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Erodium triangulare subsp. bovei include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or added to topical creams and ointments for its medicinal properties.
